summary: Iteration 3 verification of the Guillaume Girishya portfolio. All 5 backend pytest cases pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (health, contact 422 for bad email/empty name/empty message, contact happy POST + persistence GET with no _id, descending order). All Iteration-3-specific frontend assertions pass: (a) 5 project cards in the new order Founderverse / Juxprynx — Singer Website / BEARCHAT / Marriott Homes & Villas / Netflix Movie Suggestion; (b) project-0-featured-ribbon and project-1-featured-ribbon visible with Sparkles icon, while cards 2/3/4 have NO featured-ribbon testids; (c) Founderverse has demo=https://foundervers.com/ and no github link; (d) Juxprynx demo contains ggirishya.github.io/juxprynx and github contains juxprynx; (e) BEARCHAT renders

minor.issue: Endpoint is public — anyone can read every contact submission. Carryover from iter 1/2, acceptable for a personal portfolio.

critical_code_review_comments: BEARCHAT <video>: React renders `muted` as a DOM property (el.muted === true) but does NOT emit the muted HTML attribute. Functionally correct (video is muted, autoplay works), but if any external check / lint specifically asserts el.hasAttribute('muted'), it will fail. Workaround if needed: use a ref + el.setAttribute('muted','') in a useEffect, or write `muted=''` via dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Per the request's own note ('actual playback in headless Chromium not required' / 'video element exists in DOM with correct attributes'), the property-true state satisfies the intent.

Projects.jsx grid spans: with the reorder, idx-0 (Founderverse) now takes md:col-span-7 and idx-1 (Juxprynx) md:col-span-5 — both featured cards get the larger row, which is visually consistent. Cards 2/3/4 use md:col-span-4 (3-up row). Good.
Featured ribbon uses bg-[#FFB000] + black text with a glow shadow — high contrast and accessible.
server.py logger declared after route definitions still present (carried from iter 1/2). Cosmetic only.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: One TEST_-prefixed contact_message inserted via the public flow during Playwright (TEST_Iter3). Plus prior TEST_Alice from pytest run. Safe to leave or purge from contact_messages collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio_api.py (5 tests, all green). Iter-3 testids of interest: project-0-featured-ribbon, project-1-featured-ribbon, project-2-inline-video (HTMLVideoElement with src ending .mp4, properties autoplay/muted/loop/playsInline all true), project-2-demo-link label='Fullscreen demo'. Order check: titles by index = ['Founderverse','Juxprynx — Singer Website','BEARCHAT','Marriott Homes & Villas','Netflix Movie Suggestion']. Card 0 has NO github-link testid; cards 1/2/3/4 all have github-link. Note React quirk on `muted` attribute (use el.muted property, not hasAttribute('muted')).
rca of the issue: No bugs found in iteration 3. The only nuance is the React `muted` attribute reflection quirk: React applies `muted` as a JS property on the HTMLVideoElement (el.muted === true) but does not always emit it as a serialized HTML attribute. The video will still autoplay because the property is true at mount, which is what the browser actually checks. If a future automated checker insists on el.hasAttribute('muted'), set it imperatively via a ref.
